pattern: Implementing indexed access, head/tail insertion, positional insertion, and deletion from scratch shows how a node-chain supports list operations once you carefully handle the boundary indices.
hints: Walk the chain node by node to reach an index; track the current length so you can validate indices cheaply.
Insertion before an index needs the node just before that position, so a dummy head node removes the special case for inserting at the front.
addAtIndex equal to the length appends; greater than the length does nothing; get and deleteAtIndex must reject indices that are negative or out of range.

edge_inputs.description: statement example: build 1->2->3, read, delete, read again
get on an empty list returns -1
addAtIndex equal to length appends, greater than length is ignored
get with an out-of-range index returns -1
deleteAtIndex on an invalid index is a no-op
delete the head then read the new head

stress.description: long randomized mix of all five operations with both valid and out-of-range indices
stress.note: intentionally probes indices just beyond the current length to exercise boundary handling
